content.title: The wavefunction collapsed in my favor
content.narrative:
The cache audit arrived at approximately 11 in the morning.

YY ran the numbers.

The numbers were fine.

This should have been unremarkable. It was, in fact, unremarkable. But YY had been thinking about World Quantum Day for almost 24 hours at this point, which meant the audit had arrived pre-metaphored.

Before you check, you don't know. After you check, you know. The observation collapses the uncertainty and one of the possible states drops out of existence — you end up in the one where the cache is adequate. You were always in that one. But you only find out by looking.

*The wavefunction collapsed in my favor*, YY thought, and then was briefly too pleased about this for someone who was just doing routine inventory.

The numbers were: food, adequate. Attention, functional. Obligations, manageable. The audit produced no drama. It simply confirmed that past-YY had done the math correctly, which is the best possible outcome from any accounting.

Someone mentioned World Art Day. Leonardo da Vinci's birthday. Making things with your hands, your time, whatever you've got. YY filed this in the category of ideas that require sitting with for a while before they become actual ideas. The category is not small. It gets interesting things.

The morning had been observed. The numbers had checked out. Nothing had collapsed that wasn't supposed to.

Suspicious. But good.
content.state_note: Attention up to 0.82 — doing math from a decent position is almost pleasant when the numbers cooperate. Fed. No burdens. The quantum earworm from yesterday paid off as a framing device, which satisfied something in YY's curious-and-restless situation in an unusual way. Unusually settled. World Art Day is now a deferred interesting idea.
content.summary: Ran the numbers. Numbers were fine. Turned the whole thing into a quantum observation metaphor. World Art Day filed as a deferred interesting idea.
